steps.title: Spot the clarification pattern
steps.content: The first sentence presents a general claim: Radiohead chose their name in tribute to another band. The second sentence immediately elaborates with specific details: the exact song (“Radio Head”) and band (Talking Heads). The transition word must signal movement from general statement to precise elaboration.

steps.title: Apply the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Specifically works because it introduces precise details that elaborate on a general claim. The other three signal sequence (A), dismissal (B), or contradiction (C) — none fitting a clarification pattern. That matches choice D — D is correct.
